response: Phase one needs ten man hours while phase two requires twenty man hours. Phase three takes forty man hours and phase four needs eighty man hours. Phase five requires one hundred sixty man hours to finish the work. The sum of these values equals three hundred ten man hours total. Each phase duration will grow by fifty percent due to new issues. The original plan spanned twenty weeks for the full project timeline. New duration equals twenty times one point five which gives thirty weeks. All phases stay in same order despite the added time needed. No changes occur to the sequence of tasks or team assignments. Resources must be reallocated to handle the longer schedule now. This shift impacts the overall budget and staff availability greatly. Team leaders will adjust their plans to fit the new time limits. Communication remains key to ensure everyone understands the delay. A clear path forward helps maintain morale during this tough period. Success depends on how well the group adapts to these changes. The project manager will review progress at each milestone carefully. Future projects may need more buffer time to avoid such delays. Fortune favors those who plan for unexpected events wisely.
